close_reason: Implemented three lightweight syntax validation hooks: starlark-lint (buck2), toml-syntax-check (Python tomllib), json-syntax-check (Python json). Total pre-commit runtime ~24s, under 30s target.
description:
Define an even lighter test suite suitable for pre-commit hooks.

## Goals
- Must complete in <30 seconds (ideally <10 seconds)
- Run on every commit locally
- Catch obvious errors before they hit CI
- Should not require network access (no nix builds from scratch)

## Current Pre-commit Hooks
Already have:
- `nix-flake-check` - validates Nix files
- `turnkey-check` (tk check) - verifies rules.star sync
- `rust-edition-check` - Rust edition alignment
- `monorepo-dep-check` - dependency rule compliance

## Additional Candidates

### Fast Syntax Checks
- Starlark syntax validation (rules.star files)
- TOML syntax validation (*.toml files)
- JSON syntax validation (package.json, etc.)

### Fast Static Analysis
- Python type hints check (pyright/mypy on changed files only)
- Rust clippy on changed crates only (if fast enough)
- Go vet on changed packages only

### NOT Suitable for Pre-commit
- Full nix builds (too slow)
- Buck2 builds (requires full graph)
- Network-dependent operations
- Full test suites

## Implementation
- Review current hooks in devenv config
- Add new hooks that meet speed requirements
- Document which checks run when in contributing guide
- Consider `--no-verify` escape hatch documentation
title: Define lightweight pre-commit test suite
